auth_note: All endpoints except GET /system/my-permissions require ADMIN-level permission via createPermissionGuard. SUPER_ADMIN bypasses all guards. Unauthenticated requests return 401.

endpoints.description: Create a new access group for the current company (E2b.2)

test_cases.name: Create access group - happy path

body.name: Custom Role
body.description: A custom access group for testing

db_verification.query: SELECT id, code, name, company_id, is_system, is_active FROM access_groups WHERE code = 'CUSTOM_ROLE' AND company_id = '{{TEST_COMPANY_ID}}'
db_verification.expected: Exactly 1 row with matching code, name, company_id, is_system=false, is_active=true

test_cases.name: Create access group - duplicate code returns 409
Create access group - missing required code field returns 400
Create access group - missing required name field returns 400
Create access group - invalid code format (lowercase) returns 400
Create access group - STAFF user gets 403
Create access group - unauthenticated returns 401
Create access group - SUPER_ADMIN succeeds
Create access group - code exceeds max length (50 chars) returns 400

endpoints.description: Create new company (triggers access group seeding) (E2b.2 AC#6)

test_cases.name: Company creation seeds 12 default access groups

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as group_count FROM access_groups WHERE company_id = (SELECT id FROM company_profiles WHERE name = 'Test Company for Seeding') AND is_system = true
db_verification.expected: group_count = 12 (FULL_ACCESS, FINANCE_MANAGER, FINANCE_CLERK, SALES_MANAGER, SALES_STAFF, PURCHASE_MANAGER, PURCHASE_CLERK, WAREHOUSE_STAFF, HR_MANAGER, HR_VIEWER, REPORT_VIEWER, READ_ONLY)

test_cases.name: Company creation assigns FULL_ACCESS group to creating user

db_verification.query: SELECT uag.id FROM user_access_groups uag JOIN access_groups ag ON uag.access_group_id = ag.id WHERE ag.code = 'FULL_ACCESS' AND uag.user_id = '{{ADMIN_USER_ID}}' AND uag.company_id = (SELECT id FROM company_profiles WHERE name = 'Company for Assignment Test')
db_verification.expected: Exactly 1 row — creating user assigned to FULL_ACCESS group

endpoints.description: List all resources with optional filters (E2b.1)

test_cases.name: List resources - happy path returns all active resources
List resources - filter by module=system

expected.body_assertion: All items in data[] have module='system'

test_cases.name: List resources - filter by type=PAGE

expected.body_assertion: All items in data[] have type='PAGE'

test_cases.name: List resources - search by code or name

expected.body_assertion: data[] items contain 'users' in code or name (case-insensitive)

test_cases.name: List resources - STAFF user gets 403
List resources - SUPER_ADMIN gets 200
List resources - isActive=false filter

expected.body_assertion: All items in data[] have isActive=false (may be empty array)

test_cases.name: List resources - unauthenticated returns 401

endpoints.description: List access groups with pagination and filters (E2b.2)

test_cases.name: List access groups - happy path returns paginated list with userCount

expected.body_assertion: Each item in data[] has id, code, name, description, isSystem, isActive, userCount, createdAt, updatedAt

test_cases.name: List access groups - search filter

expected.body_assertion: data[] items contain 'manager' in code, name, or description

test_cases.name: List access groups - cursor pagination

expected.body_assertion: meta.hasMore=true when more than 5 groups exist, meta.cursor is set for next page

test_cases.name: List access groups - cross-company isolation (Company B groups NOT visible)

expected.body_assertion: data[] contains ONLY access groups for Company A (no Company B groups)

test_cases.name: List access groups - STAFF user gets 403
List access groups - empty result for new company

expected.body_assertion: data is empty array [], meta.total=0

endpoints.description: Get access group detail with permissions and field overrides (E2b.2)

test_cases.name: Get access group detail - happy path with permissions and fieldOverrides

expected.body_assertion: data.permissions is array of {resourceCode, canAccess, canNew, canView, canEdit, canDelete}; data.fieldOverrides is array of {resourceCode, fieldPath, visibility}

test_cases.name: Get access group detail - not found returns 404
Get access group detail - wrong company returns 404

expected.body_assertion: Other company's access group not accessible via direct ID — enforces companyId scoping

test_cases.name: Get access group detail - invalid UUID returns 400

endpoints.description: List access groups assigned to a user for the current company (E2b.3)

test_cases.name: Get user access groups - happy path returns assigned groups

expected.body_assertion: data.accessGroups is array of {id, code, name, description, isSystem, assignedBy, assignedAt}

test_cases.name: Get user access groups - user not in company returns 404
Get user access groups - user with no groups returns empty array
Get user access groups - STAFF user gets 403
Get user access groups - invalid UUID param returns 400

endpoints.description: Get current user's resolved permissions, field overrides, access groups, role, and enabled modules (E2b.4)

test_cases.name: My permissions - happy path returns resolved permissions

expected.body_assertion: data.permissions is Record<string, {canAccess, canNew, canView, canEdit, canDelete}>; data.accessGroups is array of {id, code, name}; data.enabledModules is string[]

test_cases.name: My permissions - SUPER_ADMIN gets isSuperAdmin=true and full access

expected.body_assertion: All permissions have canAccess=true, canNew=true, canView=true, canEdit=true, canDelete=true

test_cases.name: My permissions - module derivation (enabledModules from canAccess)

expected.body_assertion: data.enabledModules includes 'system' (if user has canAccess on any system.* resource); modules derived from access group permissions

test_cases.name: My permissions - unauthenticated returns 401
My permissions - STAFF user can access (any authenticated user allowed)

endpoints.description: Update access group name and/or description (E2b.2)

test_cases.name: Update access group - happy path updates name

db_verification.query: SELECT name FROM access_groups WHERE id = '{{ACCESS_GROUP_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Update access group - happy path updates description

db_verification.query: SELECT description FROM access_groups WHERE id = '{{ACCESS_GROUP_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Update access group - empty body (no fields) returns 400
Update access group - not found returns 404
Update access group - system group allows name/description update

expected.body_assertion: System groups (isSystem=true) CAN have name/description modified

endpoints.description: Replace all permissions for an access group (E2b.2)

test_cases.name: Set permissions - happy path replaces all permissions

expected.body_assertion: data is array of 2 permission objects matching the request

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as perm_count FROM access_group_permissions WHERE access_group_id = '{{ACCESS_GROUP_ID}}'
db_verification.expected: perm_count = 2 (old permissions replaced with new set)

test_cases.name: Set permissions - empty array clears all permissions

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as perm_count FROM access_group_permissions WHERE access_group_id = '{{ACCESS_GROUP_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Set permissions - invalid resource code returns 400
Set permissions - duplicate resourceCode entries returns 400
Set permissions - canAccess=false with action flags true returns 400

expected.body_assertion: Action flags must be false when canAccess is false

test_cases.name: Set permissions - access group not found returns 404

endpoints.description: Replace all access group assignments for a user (E2b.3)

test_cases.name: Assign access groups - happy path replaces all assignments

expected.body_assertion: data.accessGroups has exactly 2 items matching the provided group IDs; each has assignedBy set to admin's userId

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as assignment_count FROM user_access_groups WHERE user_id = '{{TEST_USER_ID}}' AND company_id = '{{TEST_COMPANY_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Assign access groups - empty array returns 422 (business rule: min 1 group)
Assign access groups - group from other company returns 400
Assign access groups - non-existent group ID returns 400
Assign access groups - user not in company returns 404
Assign access groups - inactive group returns 400
Assign access groups - STAFF user gets 403
Assign access groups - duplicate group IDs in array returns 400
Assign access groups - records correct assignedBy

expected.body_assertion: data.accessGroups[0].assignedBy equals the admin user's ID

db_verification.query: SELECT assigned_by FROM user_access_groups WHERE user_id = '{{TEST_USER_ID}}' AND company_id = '{{TEST_COMPANY_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Delete access group - happy path soft-deletes custom group with no users

expected.body_assertion: Empty response body

db_verification.query: SELECT is_active FROM access_groups WHERE id = '{{CUSTOM_GROUP_NO_USERS_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Delete access group - system group returns 409 Conflict

db_verification.query: SELECT is_active FROM access_groups WHERE id = '{{SYSTEM_GROUP_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Delete access group - group with active users returns 409
Delete access group - not found returns 404
Delete access group - STAFF user gets 403
Delete access group - unauthenticated returns 401

endpoints.description: Permission guard enforcement tests using resource routes (E2b.4)

test_cases.name: Permission guard - blocks user WITHOUT canAccess on resource

expected.body_assertion: User has no access groups or no canAccess on system.resources.list -> 403

test_cases.name: Permission guard - allows user WITH canAccess on resource
Permission guard - action-level check: canAccess=true but canDelete=false -> DELETE returns 403

expected.body_assertion: User has canAccess=true, canView=true but canDelete=false on system.access-groups.detail -> DELETE blocked

test_cases.name: Permission guard - SUPER_ADMIN bypasses all checks (no access groups needed)

expected.body_assertion: SUPER_ADMIN accesses resource without any access group assignment

test_cases.name: Permission guard - most-permissive-wins across multiple groups

expected.body_assertion: User in Group A (canView=false on system.resources.list) AND Group B (canView=true) -> OR merge -> canView=true -> allowed

endpoints.description: Permission cache invalidation tests after group permission edit (E2b.4)

test_cases.name: Cache invalidation - after permission update, new permission applies immediately

expected.body_assertion: After this mutation, a user in this group calling GET /system/resources should now get 403 (permission cache invalidated via accessGroup.updated event)

test_cases.name: Cache invalidation - after user group reassignment, new permissions apply immediately

expected.body_assertion: After this mutation, calling GET /system/resources as TEST_USER should reflect READ_ONLY permissions (cache invalidated via user.accessGroups.assigned event)

endpoints.description: Field-level visibility filtering tests via onSend hook (E2b.5)

test_cases.name: Field filter - HIDDEN field stripped from response

expected.body_assertion: Field with HIDDEN override (e.g., vatNumber) is completely absent from data object

test_cases.name: Field filter - READ_ONLY field present with _fieldMeta marker

expected.body_assertion: Field with READ_ONLY override present in data; _fieldMeta contains {fieldName: 'readOnly'}

test_cases.name: Field filter - SUPER_ADMIN bypass (no fields stripped, no _fieldMeta)

expected.body_assertion: All fields present; no _fieldMeta property (SUPER_ADMIN bypasses field filtering entirely)

test_cases.name: Field filter - no override defaults to VISIBLE (all fields present)

expected.body_assertion: All fields present; no _fieldMeta (no field overrides = default VISIBLE)

test_cases.name: Field filter - most-permissive-wins: Group A HIDDEN + Group B VISIBLE = VISIBLE

expected.body_assertion: User in Group A (field HIDDEN) + Group B (field VISIBLE) -> field is present in response (VISIBLE wins)

endpoints.description: Export company defaults as JSON (E2b.6 - NOT YET IMPLEMENTED)

test_cases.name: Export defaults - happy path returns JSON with all entity types

expected.body_assertion: data.resources is array of active resources; data.accessGroups includes permissions[] and fieldOverrides[] per group; data.version matches company-defaults.json format

test_cases.name: Export defaults - non-ADMIN gets 403
Export defaults - SUPER_ADMIN succeeds

endpoints.description: Import company defaults from JSON (E2b.6 - NOT YET IMPLEMENTED)

test_cases.name: Import defaults - happy path returns APPLIED status with summary

db_verification.query: SELECT id FROM access_groups WHERE code = 'IMPORTED_GROUP' AND company_id = '{{TEST_COMPANY_ID}}'

test_cases.name: Import defaults - dry run returns DRY_RUN status without persisting

db_verification.query: SELECT id FROM access_groups WHERE code = 'DRY_RUN_GROUP' AND company_id = '{{TEST_COMPANY_ID}}'
db_verification.expected: 0 rows — dry run did NOT persist

test_cases.name: Import defaults - unsupported version returns 400
Import defaults - non-ADMIN gets 403
Import defaults - export then import round-trip fidelity

expected.body_assertion: Re-exporting after import produces identical JSON (round-trip fidelity)

cross_cutting_test_scenarios.description: Test scenarios that span multiple endpoints and verify cross-cutting concerns

scenarios.name: Full RBAC lifecycle: create group -> set permissions -> assign user -> verify enforcement
steps: 2. PUT /system/access-groups/:id/permissions -> grant canAccess+canView on system.resources.list only
3. PUT /system/users/:id/access-groups -> assign CUSTOM_TEST to test user
5. POST /system/access-groups (as test user) -> 403 (canNew NOT granted on system.access-groups.list)
6. GET /system/my-permissions (as test user) -> verify permissions reflect CUSTOM_TEST grants

scenarios.name: Permission cache invalidation lifecycle
steps: 3. GET /system/resources (as same user) -> 403 (cache invalidated, new permission applied)

scenarios.name: Most-permissive-wins across multiple groups end-to-end
steps: 1. Create Group A: canView=false on system.resources.list
2. Create Group B: canView=true on system.resources.list

scenarios.name: Company creation seeds 12 groups with permissions and assigns creator
steps: 2. GET /system/access-groups (in new company context) -> verify 12 system groups

scenarios.name: Field-level visibility end-to-end with permission guard integration
steps: 1. Create access group with field override: vatNumber=HIDDEN on system.company-profile.detail
3. GET /system/company-profile (as user) -> verify vatNumber absent from response

not_implemented.note: E2b-6 (export/import) is ready-for-dev but NOT implemented. Test cases are included as planned tests.

key_risks_tested: R-004: Permission cache stale data (2 tests)
R-008: Cross-company access group leakage (3 tests)
